concept: Redundant / Having Independent Alternative Means for a Function
description: Of a technical arrangement, having two or more sufficiently independent components, paths, stores, or methods intended to preserve a stated function when one is unavailable or fails. By the quality-noun rule the word names protective redundancy. Redundancy does not mean needless repetition, identical duplication, guaranteed reliability, spare capacity, diversity, or resilience under every shared cause.
sound_symbolism: Relational 'weso' opens toward more than one path, while 'panu' layers support beneath a shared function. The syllables remain distinct, reflecting alternatives that should not collapse into one point of failure.
grammatical_notes: An adjective placed before a noun or used predicatively: 'wesopanu terulora' (a redundant system); 'ha terulora wesopanu nai.' (This system is redundant.) The quality-noun rule supplies redundancy. State the preserved function and independence assumptions. Distinct from 'phowe' (share), 'thuroi' (resilient), and unnecessary duplication.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Independent alternatives can preserve essential shared functions through repair, scarcity, and changing conditions without promising invulnerability.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Communities often maintain several routes, tools, stores, or skills so one loss does not erase a necessary practice.

tags.activity: continued function through an alternate component, path, or method
tags.community: a resilience practice for shared infrastructure and services
tags.creation: a design quality based on independent alternative means
